name: Crepes - to Make and Store
recipeInstructions:
1. In a blender or food processor, combine the cold water, whole milk, eggs, salt, and flour. With a rubber spatula, scrape down any batter that adheres to the sides of the processor.
2. If the batter appears thick, add more water; if too thin, gradually whisk in more flour until the desired consistency is achieved.
3. Set aside the batter in the refrigerator for at least an hour.
4. Rub a 7-inch crepe pan or non-stick pan with vegetable oil or melted butter. Place over high heat and allow the pan to smoke slightly.
5. Ladle about 3 tablespoons of batter into the hot pan, lifting it from the heat. Swirl the batter around so that it evenly coats the bottom. Pour any excess batter back into the bowl for the next crepe.
6. Let the crepe cook on one side for about a minute or until it looks dried out. Flip the crepe over and cook for 30 seconds on the other side. Repeat until all are done.
7. To store, stack the crepes with waxed paper or parchment squares in between each one and freeze in batches.
Yield: about 12 crepes
